chi: noun. The twenty-second letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets.
child: noun. A person who has not yet reached adulthood, whether natural (puberty), cultural (initiation), or legal (majority, A person yet a
childish: adjective. Immature in thought or behaviour.
chili: noun. The pungent, spicy fresh or dried fruit of any of several cultivated varieties of capsicum peppers, used in cooking.
cis: adjective. Having two mutations on two genes on the same chromosome of a homologous pair.
disc: noun. A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.
dish: noun. A vessel such as a plate for holding or serving food, often flat with a depressed region in the middle.
hic: interjection. An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
lich: noun. A reanimated corpse or undead being; particularly an intelligent, undead spellcaster.
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
sic: adverb. Thus; as written; used to indicate, for example, that text is being quoted as it is from the source.
sild: noun. Any young herring (other than a sprat), especially if canned and processed in Scandinavia for sale as a sardine.
slid: verb. simple past and past participle of slide
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
dich: noun. Obsolete form of ditch (“a trench”).
hld: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Halang Doan.
ihi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ihievbe.
lish: noun. A hybrid variety of English having a name that is derived from a portmanteau of the name of one language and the word English, such
lsd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lishana Deni.
shchi: noun. A type of soup from Russia made from cabbage.
sich: noun. An administrative and military centre for the Zaporozhian and Danube Cossacks.
sil: noun. A yellowish pigment used by painters in ancient times.
